reasoning: The base rate for swimming world records surviving short horizons is very high (0.991). Bobby Finke set the 1500m long course (LCM) world record at the Paris 2024 Olympics (14:30.67), breaking a legendary 12-year-old mark. The Wikipedia page as of November 4, 2025, confirms he remains the sole record holder, meaning his record survived the July 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore. The forecast horizon falls entirely within the typical short course (SCM) season (the SC World Championships take place in December 2025), a period during which practically no high-profile long course events are held, let alone targeted by elite athletes to break grueling 1500m records. Between the lack of upcoming LCM taper meets and the fact that Finke is leaps ahead of almost all competitors, the record is incredibly safe. Probabilities are capped at 0.95 as per instructions, and step down slightly to reflect the slightly larger horizon by early 2026.
